# The Bhagavad Gītā

> The Bhagavad Gītā in modern English with multi-tradition commentary on every verse — Advaita, Bhakti, Dvaita, Vishishtadvaita, Kashmir Shaivism, and more. Free, ad-free, and downloadable as a book.

## Book

- [The Bhagavad Gītā — Free PDF Book (English) | bhagavad.net](https://bhagavad.net/book): The Bhagavad Gītā — Free book edition in English. All 18 chapters with Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, English translation, and multi-tradition synthesis. Read online or download as PDF.

## Chapter

- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 1: Arjuna's Dilemma — All 47 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/1): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 1 (अर्जुनविषादयोग, Arjuna's Dilemma) — On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna sees his relatives, teachers, and friends on both sides. Overwhelmed by sorrow and moral confusion, he drops his bow and refuses to fight. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 10: Divine Manifestations — All 42 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/10): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 10 (विभूतियोग, Divine Manifestations) — Krishna describes his divine manifestations throughout creation — the best, the most powerful, the most beautiful in every category. Everything extraordinary is a spark of his splendor. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 11: The Cosmic Vision — All 55 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/11): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 11 (विश्वरूपदर्शनयोग, The Cosmic Vision) — Arjuna is granted divine vision to behold Krishna's universal form — infinite, all-encompassing, terrifying and beautiful. He sees all of creation, destruction, and time itself. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 12: The Path of Devotion — All 20 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/12): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 12 (भक्तियोग, The Path of Devotion) — Krishna declares devotion as the supreme path and describes the qualities of his dearest devotees: compassionate, humble, content, forgiving, and unwavering in faith. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 13: The Field & The Knower — All 34 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/13): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 13 (क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञविभागयोग, The Field & The Knower) — The distinction between the body (the field) and the soul (the knower of the field). Krishna explains the elements of material nature and the qualities that lead to true knowledge. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 14: The Three Gunas — All 27 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/14): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 14 (गुणत्रयविभागयोग, The Three Gunas) — The three qualities of material nature — sattva (goodness), rajas (passion), and tamas (ignorance) — and how they bind the soul. Krishna teaches how to transcend all three. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 15: The Supreme Person — All 20 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/15): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 15 (पुरुषोत्तमयोग, The Supreme Person) — The metaphor of the cosmic banyan tree, rooted above and branching below. Krishna reveals himself as the Supreme Person, beyond both the perishable and imperishable. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 16: Divine & Demonic Natures — All 24 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/16): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 16 (दैवासुरसम्पद्विभागयोग, Divine & Demonic Natures) — Two types of human tendencies: divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (arrogance, anger, ignorance). Krishna urges cultivation of divine nature. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 17: Three Kinds of Faith — All 28 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/17): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 17 (श्रद्धात्रयविभागयोग, Three Kinds of Faith) — Faith itself comes in three types, shaped by the gunas. Krishna examines how food, worship, austerity, and charity differ based on whether they arise from goodness, passion, or ignorance. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 18: Liberation Through Surrender — All 78 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/18): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 18 (मोक्षसंन्यासयोग, Liberation Through Surrender) — The grand conclusion: Krishna summarizes all paths, reveals his most confidential teaching, and gives Arjuna the freedom to choose. Arjuna's doubt is destroyed; he is ready to act. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 2: The Path of Knowledge — All 72 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/2): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 2 (सांख्ययोग, The Path of Knowledge) — Krishna begins his teaching: the soul is eternal, the body is temporary. He introduces the core philosophy of the Gita, including detachment from results and the nature of a person of steady wisdom. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 3: The Path of Action — All 43 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/3): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 3 (कर्मयोग, The Path of Action) — Krishna explains why action is superior to inaction. One must perform duties without attachment to results, maintaining equanimity in success and failure. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 4: Knowledge & Renunciation — All 42 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/4): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 4 (ज्ञानकर्मसंन्यासयोग, Knowledge & Renunciation) — Krishna reveals the ancient lineage of this teaching and explains how divine knowledge destroys the bondage of karma. He describes various forms of sacrifice and spiritual practice. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 5: Renunciation of Action — All 29 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/5): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 5 (कर्मसंन्यासयोग, Renunciation of Action) — The paths of renunciation and selfless action are compared. Krishna teaches that true renunciation is internal — acting without ego and desire while remaining engaged in the world. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 6: The Path of Meditation — All 47 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/6): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 6 (ध्यानयोग, The Path of Meditation) — Detailed instructions on meditation practice: posture, focus, diet, and lifestyle. Krishna addresses what happens to those who begin the spiritual path but do not complete it in one lifetime. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 7: Knowledge & Realization — All 30 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/7): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 7 (ज्ञानविज्ञानयोग, Knowledge & Realization) — Krishna reveals his divine nature — both the material and spiritual dimensions of reality. He explains why few truly know him and describes four types of seekers. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 8: The Imperishable Absolute — All 28 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/8): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 8 (अक्षरब्रह्मयोग, The Imperishable Absolute) — The nature of the imperishable Brahman, the process of death and rebirth, and how one's final thoughts determine the next destination. Krishna teaches the path beyond return. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.
- [Bhagavad Gita Chapter 9: Royal Knowledge — All 34 Verses with Multi-Tradition Commentary | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/chapter/9): Bhagavad Gītā Chapter 9 (राजविद्याराजगुह्ययोग, Royal Knowledge) — The most confidential knowledge: Krishna pervades all creation yet remains transcendent. Simple devotion — offering a leaf, flower, or water with love — reaches the divine directly. Read every verse in Sanskrit, IAST transliteration, modern English translation, and 8 commentary traditions.

- [Learn Sanskrit in 14 Days | The Bhagavad Gītā](https://bhagavad.net/learn-sanskrit): A 14-day Sanskrit course built around Bhagavad Gītā verses — learn to read Devanagari and understand the original text.
